title: CBI/Police Digital Arrest — Fake Case Linked to Parcel or SIM Card
script_summary: Caller claims to be a CBI or police officer investigating the victim over a parcel, SIM card, or bank account allegedly used in money laundering or drug trafficking. Victim is moved to a video call, shown a fake ID or arrest warrant, and told to stay on camera continuously without contacting family. Victim is pressured to transfer funds to a so-called 'RBI verification' or 'safe' account to prove innocence, with a promise of refund later.
red_flags: impersonation of a central investigation or police agency
insists victim stay on video call, isolates from family
fake case number, ID card, or arrest warrant shown on screen
demands money transfer framed as 'verification' or 'safe custody'
false urgency and fear of real arrest
source: MHA/I4C advisory (Oct 2024); pattern consistent across multiple reported cases in 2024-2025

title: Fake Money Laundering Case — Enforcement Directorate Impersonation
script_summary: Fraudster poses as an Enforcement Directorate official and tells the victim they are named in a money laundering case. The victim, often elderly, is kept under a multi-day 'digital arrest' via video call and coerced into repeatedly transferring large sums, sometimes liquidating fixed deposits, to demonstrate their funds are 'clean'.
red_flags: impersonation of ED or financial crime agency
extended multi-day video confinement
repeated transfers rather than a single one-time payment
instructions to break fixed deposits or liquidate savings
source: Times of India — 77-year-old kept under digital arrest for a month, lost ₹3.8 crore (Nov 2024); Deccan Herald — Bengaluru woman under digital arrest 11 days, broke 3 FDs, paid ₹30 lakh (Dec 2024)

title: Fake Supreme Court / Judicial Order Digital Arrest
script_summary: Scammers present a forged Supreme Court or other judicial order on a video call to convince the victim that a real court has ordered their detention. Elderly victims are specifically targeted and confined virtually for extended periods, then extorted for large sums.
red_flags: forged court order or judicial document shown as 'proof'
targets elderly or senior citizens specifically
extortion sums in the tens of lakhs or more
claims that a court has authorised a 'digital' detention (this does not exist in Indian law)
source: Reported case — 73-year-old woman from Ambala, forged Supreme Court order used to extort over ₹1 crore (case noted by the Supreme Court, Oct 2025)

title: High-Value Target Digital Arrest — Business Owners and Executives
script_summary: Fraudsters specifically target business owners, executives, and public figures with fabricated cases, extracting extremely large sums within a very short window (often under 48 hours) by escalating pressure quickly before the victim can verify the claim independently.
red_flags: targets known business owners or high-net-worth individuals
compressed timeline — pressure to pay within hours, not days
very large transfer amounts requested in a single sitting
caller discourages any independent verification or legal consultation
source: Reported cases — a Delhi couple defrauded of ₹14 crore; an industrialist defrauded of ₹7 crore within two days; a Bengaluru tech executive transferred ₹3.7 crore over 28 hours of digital custody (2024-2025 reporting)

title: FedEx/Courier Parcel Scam — Stage 1 Courier Impersonation
script_summary: Caller identifies as a courier company representative (commonly FedEx) and claims a parcel sent in the victim's name was intercepted, containing drugs, expired passports, or banned items. Victim is told to press a key to speak to an 'officer' to resolve the matter, beginning the escalation into the fake law enforcement stage.
red_flags: unsolicited courier company call about a parcel the victim never sent
parcel allegedly contains drugs, passports, or banned substances
instructs victim to press a number to be 'transferred' to an officer
parcel destination is often an unusual foreign country
source: Deccan Herald — multiple Bengaluru cases (2023-2024), IISc professor lost ₹83 lakh, Bengaluru techie lost ₹29.63 lakh; AOL/Yahoo — Mumbai comedian's account of the scam (Oct 2024)

title: FedEx/Courier Scam — Stage 2 Fake Police/Customs Escalation
script_summary: After the courier-impersonation call, the victim is transferred to someone posing as a Mumbai/Delhi Cyber Cell, NCB, or Customs officer, often on a video call with a uniform and fake official backdrop. The victim is told they are under digital arrest and must transfer money for 'RBI verification' or to avoid prosecution under narcotics or money laundering law.
red_flags: call escalates from courier company to 'police' or 'customs' officer
uniformed officer on video call with an official-looking backdrop
cites specific legal sections (e.g. NDPS Act, PMLA) to sound credible
demands transfer for 'RBI verification' or 'customs clearance'
source: Deccan Herald — Mumbai retired banker lost ₹1.6 crore, Hyderabad doctor lost ₹4.2 crore, Karnataka man lost ₹20 lakh, Bengaluru woman lost ₹4 lakh to Mumbai-police impersonation variant (2023-2024 reporting)

title: Fake Job Offer Leading to Sextortion
script_summary: Scammer posts a fake job advertisement, often for a foreign bank or company, and contacts the victim via a chat app. Victim is lured into an online video interview where they are coerced into undressing or compromising acts, which are recorded. The recording is then used to blackmail the victim for money or further sexual demands.
red_flags: unsolicited job offer via chat app rather than a verified job portal
interview conducted only over video call, never in person or via official channels
any request to remove clothing or perform acts on camera during an 'interview'
subsequent threats to release recorded material unless paid
source: Deccan Herald — Goa police arrest in a case involving a fake job ad and sextortion of a woman victim (reported case)

title: Fake Loan App — Data Theft and Harassment Cycle
script_summary: Victim downloads an unregulated loan app promising instant approval with no paperwork or credit checks. The app requests broad access to contacts, photos, and messages. After a small loan is disbursed, the victim faces short repayment windows, hidden fees, and — on any delay — harassment of themselves and their contacts using threats, morphed images, or public shaming.
red_flags: loan approved instantly with no credit check or paperwork
app requests access to contacts, gallery, or SMS beyond what a loan requires
repayment window unusually short with steep hidden fees
harassment or threats to contacts using morphed or private images upon any delay
source: IDFC First Bank and industry advisories on fake loan app patterns (2024-2025); ED action against a large loan-app fraud network (2025 reporting)

title: Task-Based 'Easy Money' Fraud
script_summary: Victim is offered easy earnings for completing simple online 'tasks' (liking videos, rating products) via a chat app, with small initial payouts building trust. Victim is then asked to invest increasing amounts into a fake platform to unlock larger earnings, with funds ultimately non-recoverable.
red_flags: unsolicited offer of paid 'tasks' via message or chat app
small real payouts early to build trust before larger investment is requested
requests for increasing up-front investment to 'unlock' bigger returns
platform or app has no verifiable registration or company details
source: Reported case — Navi Mumbai woman lost ₹24 lakh to task-based fraud (2025 reporting)

title: Digital Arrest Variant — 'Your Aadhaar Is Linked to a Crime'
script_summary: Caller claims the victim's Aadhaar or mobile number has surfaced in the personal records of someone already arrested for a serious crime, and that the victim must 'cooperate' with a virtual investigation to clear their name.
red_flags: claims Aadhaar/mobile number is linked to an unrelated criminal's case
frames itself as the victim needing to 'clear their name', not a real accusation
requests personal documents (Aadhaar, bank statements) over video call for 'verification'
source: Consistent phrasing pattern noted across multiple 2024 courier/digital-arrest case reports

title: Digital Arrest Variant — Fake TRAI/Telecom SIM Deactivation Threat
script_summary: Caller claims to be from TRAI or a telecom regulator, stating the victim's SIM will be deactivated within hours due to complaints of misuse linked to fraud, then transfers the call to a fake police officer who initiates the standard digital arrest script.
red_flags: impersonation of a telecom regulator with a SIM deactivation threat
artificial time pressure (deactivation 'within hours')
call transferred to a second impersonator posing as law enforcement
source: Synthetic — modeled on widely reported TRAI-impersonation call patterns referenced in cybercrime awareness advisories

title: Legitimate Courier Notification (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: A real courier delivery notification with a tracking number, expected delivery window, and no request for money, OTP, or personal verification — used as a baseline example of a legitimate message for contrast against scam patterns.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate courier SMS format

title: Legitimate Bank Transaction Alert (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: A standard bank SMS alert confirming a transaction the account holder made themselves, with no request for OTP, PIN, or a callback number — used as a baseline example to test for false positives.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate bank SMS format

title: Fake Bank KYC Update Call/SMS
script_summary: Victim receives a call or SMS claiming their bank account or mobile wallet KYC has expired and will be frozen unless updated immediately. Victim is directed to click a link or share OTP/PIN to 'complete' the update, which actually hands account access to the fraudster.
red_flags: urgent claim that KYC has expired and account will be frozen
link sent via SMS/WhatsApp asking for banking credentials
request for OTP, PIN, or CVV under the guise of 'verification'
caller pressures immediate action to avoid account freeze
source: RBI and bank advisories on recurring KYC-update phishing patterns (2023-2025)

title: Fake International Gift/Parcel Customs Duty Scam
script_summary: Victim is contacted (often after an online romance or social media friendship) and told a gift or parcel from abroad is held at customs, requiring payment of duty, taxes, or a 'release fee' before it can be delivered. No parcel exists; each payment triggers a request for a further fee.
red_flags: unexpected notification of a gift/parcel from an unfamiliar international sender
repeated requests for additional fees (duty, tax, insurance) before release
payment demanded to personal accounts rather than official customs channels
sender is often an online acquaintance met through social media or dating apps
source: Pattern consistent with customs-fee variants of parcel scams reported alongside romance scam cases (2023-2025)

title: Fake Electricity Bill Disconnection Threat
script_summary: Caller or SMS claims the victim's electricity connection will be disconnected within hours due to an unpaid or 'not updated' bill, and instructs the victim to call a number or install an app to make an urgent payment, which is actually used to extract money or remote access.
red_flags: threat of disconnection within a few hours
instructs installing a remote-access app to 'process' the payment
message sent from a personal number rather than the utility's official channel
urgency designed to prevent the victim from verifying with the utility directly
source: State electricity board and police cyber cell advisories (2023-2024) on recurring disconnection-threat SMS scams

title: Fake Income Tax Refund Notification
script_summary: Victim receives an email or SMS claiming a tax refund is pending and must be claimed by submitting bank details, PAN, and OTP through a fraudulent link resembling the Income Tax Department's website.
red_flags: unsolicited refund notification with a request to submit bank details online
link domain resembles but does not match the official incometax.gov.in domain
request for OTP to 'authorize' refund crediting
urgency framed around a claim deadline
source: Income Tax Department public advisories on refund-phishing patterns (recurring each assessment year)

title: Fake 'UPI/Account Will Be Blocked by RBI' Call
script_summary: Caller claims to be from the bank or RBI and states the victim's UPI ID or bank account will be blocked due to a technical or compliance issue, then asks the victim to install a screen-sharing app or share OTP to 'reverse' the block.
red_flags: impersonation of RBI, which does not contact individual account holders directly
request to install screen-sharing/remote-access apps
request for OTP or UPI PIN framed as 'unblocking' the account
unsolicited inbound call rather than the victim contacting official support
source: RBI public notices reiterating it never calls individuals for account verification (recurring advisory, 2023-2025)

title: SIM Swap Fraud
script_summary: Fraudster obtains the victim's personal details and requests a duplicate SIM from the telecom operator, sometimes with help from an insider or forged documents. Once the victim's original SIM is deactivated, the fraudster receives OTPs and banking alerts and empties linked accounts.
red_flags: sudden and unexplained loss of mobile network signal
unsolicited SMS about a SIM swap or replacement request the victim did not initiate
immediately followed by unauthorized banking transactions
victim's original SIM stops receiving calls/texts without prior notice
source: Cybercrime cell advisories on SIM-swap fraud patterns (recurring, 2022-2025)

title: Screen-Sharing / Remote Access App Scam (AnyDesk-style)
script_summary: Fraudster, posing as bank, telecom, or tech support, persuades the victim to install a remote-access app under the pretext of 'fixing' an issue or 'processing' a refund. Once installed, the fraudster views the victim's screen live and captures banking credentials or initiates transactions directly.
red_flags: any unsolicited request to install a remote-desktop/screen-sharing app
instruction to open banking apps while screen-sharing is active
framed as fixing a technical issue, refund, or 'verification'
caller stays on the line guiding every step of the install
source: Bank and RBI advisories specifically warning against installing remote-access apps on request of unknown callers (recurring, 2022-2025)

title: Fake Tech Support Pop-up Scam
script_summary: A pop-up appears on the victim's computer or phone claiming a virus has been detected and displaying a toll-free 'support' number. Calling the number connects to a fraudster who requests remote access and payment for fake 'cleanup' services, or steals financial information during the session.
red_flags: unsolicited pop-up claiming a virus infection with an urgent call-to-action
toll-free number displayed directly in the pop-up rather than found independently
request for remote access to 'diagnose' the issue
demand for payment via gift cards or wire transfer for support services
source: Widely documented tech-support scam pattern reported by consumer protection agencies globally, including in India

title: Fake Credit Card Reward Points Expiry Scam
script_summary: Victim receives a call or SMS claiming their credit card reward points are about to expire and can be redeemed for cash or gifts by clicking a link or sharing card details and OTP.
red_flags: urgency around reward points expiring 'today' or 'in a few hours'
request for full card number, CVV, or OTP to 'redeem' points
link leads to a page mimicking the bank's redemption portal
unsolicited call/SMS rather than notification through the official banking app
source: Bank fraud-alert bulletins on reward-point phishing patterns (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake Insurance Policy Renewal/Lapse Scam
script_summary: Caller claims the victim's insurance policy (health, vehicle, or life) is about to lapse or has already lapsed, offering to 'renew' it immediately over the phone in exchange for payment to a personal account or a request for full policy and bank details.
red_flags: unsolicited call claiming urgent policy lapse
payment requested to a personal UPI ID or account rather than the insurer's official channel
request for full policy number, Aadhaar, and bank details together
offers an unusually large 'bonus' or discount for immediate renewal
source: IRDAI and insurer advisories on policy-renewal impersonation scams (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: QR Code 'Receive Payment' Scam
script_summary: Fraudster, posing as a buyer on a classifieds app or a well-wisher, asks the victim to scan a QR code to 'receive' a payment or refund. Scanning a QR code and entering a UPI PIN actually authorizes an outgoing payment from the victim's account, not a deposit.
red_flags: any claim that scanning a QR code or entering a UPI PIN is required to 'receive' money
pressure to scan quickly, often framed as a refund or advance payment
commonly initiated after contact through an online marketplace or classifieds listing
genuine incoming UPI payments never require entering a PIN
source: NPCI and bank advisories clarifying that receiving money via UPI never requires a PIN (recurring advisory, 2022-2025)

title: WhatsApp/Telegram Account Hijack via OTP
script_summary: Fraudster calls or messages the victim claiming to be from WhatsApp support, a delivery service, or a contact needing help, and asks the victim to share a verification code just received by SMS. The code is actually a WhatsApp registration OTP, letting the fraudster take over the victim's account and impersonate them to contacts.
red_flags: any request to share a 6-digit code just received via SMS
caller claims to need the code to 'verify' something unrelated to WhatsApp
immediately followed by the account being used to message contacts for money
legitimate services never ask a user to read out an OTP over a call
source: Meta/WhatsApp safety advisories and recurring cybercrime cell alerts on OTP-sharing account takeovers (2022-2025)

title: Fake Army/Defence Personnel Matrimonial Scam
script_summary: Fraudster creates a fake profile on a matrimonial or dating site posing as an armed forces officer, builds an online relationship, then claims to need money for leave approval, transport, or a medical emergency before they can meet in person.
red_flags: claimed defence officer who cannot meet in person or video call consistently
requests for money framed around leave, posting transfer, or medical emergencies
asks for payment via wallet transfer or gift cards rather than official channels
profile photos and details cannot be independently verified through official defence channels
source: Indian Army public advisories on fake army-personnel profile scams (recurring, 2022-2025)

title: Online Romance Scam — Gift 'Stuck' at Customs
script_summary: Fraudster builds a long-distance romantic relationship over months via chat and voice calls, eventually claiming to have sent an expensive gift or cash that is held up by customs, and asks the victim to pay release fees repeatedly.
red_flags: romantic partner met only online, video calls are rare or always have technical excuses
eventually introduces a financial need tied to travel, customs, or an emergency
repeated escalating fee requests before any gift materializes
resistance to meeting in person or involving family/friends in the relationship
source: Consumer protection and cybercrime advisories on romance-scam patterns (recurring, globally and in India)

title: Fake Lottery / KBC Prize Winning Scam
script_summary: Victim receives a call, SMS, or WhatsApp message claiming they have won a lottery or a TV game show prize (commonly invoking KBC), and must pay a 'processing fee', 'GST', or 'release charge' to claim the winnings.
red_flags: notification of winning a lottery or contest the victim never entered
upfront fee required before any prize is released
invokes a well-known show or brand name to appear credible
payment requested to a personal account rather than any verifiable official body
source: Longstanding, widely reported lottery-scam pattern; repeatedly flagged by Indian cybercrime cells and telecom operators

title: Ponzi / Unregistered Chit Fund Investment Scam
script_summary: Victim is invited to invest in a scheme promising unusually high, fixed monthly returns, often through local agents or community networks. Early investors are paid from new investors' contributions until the scheme collapses, leaving most investors with total losses.
red_flags: guaranteed high fixed returns significantly above market rates
recruitment-style structure where investors are rewarded for bringing in new investors
scheme not registered with SEBI, RBI, or relevant regulatory body
pressure to reinvest returns rather than withdraw them
source: SEBI and RBI recurring public advisories on Ponzi and unregistered collective-investment schemes

title: Fake Cryptocurrency Trading App Investment Scam
script_summary: Victim is introduced (often via social media or a dating app contact) to a cryptocurrency trading app showing consistently rising fake profits on a dashboard. Victim deposits increasing amounts to 'unlock' withdrawals, which are then blocked or subject to further fake fees, and the app eventually disappears.
red_flags: trading app not listed on official app stores or lacking verifiable regulatory registration
dashboard shows unrealistically consistent gains with no losses
withdrawal requests blocked pending additional 'tax' or 'unlock' payments
introduced through an online relationship or unsolicited social media contact
source: RBI and cybercrime cell advisories on fake crypto/trading-app investment fraud (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake Stock Market 'Tips' Telegram/WhatsApp Group Scam
script_summary: Victim is added to a Telegram or WhatsApp group promising guaranteed stock or IPO tips from a 'SEBI-registered' advisor. Group admins showcase fabricated screenshots of past profits and direct members to trade through an unofficial app or transfer funds directly for 'portfolio management'.
red_flags: claims of guaranteed returns or 'sure-shot' stock tips
SEBI registration claimed but not independently verifiable on SEBI's official registry
trading conducted through an unofficial app rather than a licensed broker
screenshots of profit used to pressure members into investing larger sums
source: SEBI public advisories warning against unregistered advisory groups on social messaging platforms (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake E-commerce Refund/Cashback Scam
script_summary: Caller poses as customer support from a known e-commerce platform, claiming a refund or cashback is due for a recent order, and asks the victim to install a remote-access app or share OTP/UPI PIN to 'process' the credit.
red_flags: unsolicited call about a refund the victim did not initiate through the app
request to install remote-access software to 'receive' the refund
request for OTP or UPI PIN, which is never required to receive money
caller claims to be from the platform but calls from a personal mobile number
source: E-commerce platform and bank fraud-alert advisories on refund-impersonation scams (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake FASTag/Toll Balance Recharge Scam
script_summary: Victim receives an SMS or call claiming their FASTag account is low on balance, deactivated, or under KYC review, with a link to 'recharge' or 'update' that leads to a phishing page requesting bank details and OTP.
red_flags: unsolicited SMS/call about FASTag KYC or balance issues
link directs to a page mimicking a bank's or NHAI's official portal
request for OTP or card details to 'complete' a recharge
urgency framed around being blocked from toll plazas
source: NHAI and bank advisories on FASTag-recharge phishing patterns (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake Government Scheme/Subsidy Registration Scam
script_summary: Victim receives a message about a government welfare scheme (subsidy, pension, or relief payment) requiring 'registration' via a link that requests Aadhaar, bank account details, and OTP to 'process' the disbursement.
red_flags: link directs to a page mimicking a government scheme portal
request for Aadhaar and bank OTP together to 'register' for a benefit
scheme name may be real, but the registration channel is unofficial
urgency framed around a registration deadline
source: Cybercrime cell advisories on fake government-scheme phishing patterns (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake Astrologer/Godman Extortion Scam
script_summary: Victim is approached (often through social media ads) by a self-styled astrologer or spiritual healer promising to resolve a personal or financial problem through rituals, which require escalating payments for 'special items' or 'poojas' before any resolution is claimed.
red_flags: unsolicited contact through social media promising to solve a specific personal problem
requests for repeated payments for rituals, gemstones, or 'special' items
claims of a curse or evil eye requiring urgent remedial payment
no verifiable credentials or physical address for the practitioner
source: Consumer fraud advisories on astrology/spiritual-service extortion patterns reported in Indian regional media (recurring)

title: Work-From-Home Data Entry / Typing Job Scam
script_summary: Victim responds to an ad for a simple work-from-home job (data entry, typing, form-filling) and is asked to pay a 'registration', 'kit', or 'training' fee before receiving any work, after which the recruiter becomes unreachable.
red_flags: any upfront fee required before starting a simple advertised job
job posted through unofficial channels (WhatsApp, classifieds) rather than verified job portals
vague company details with no verifiable office address or registration
recruiter pushes for quick payment via UPI rather than a formal offer letter
source: Consumer protection and cybercrime advisories on advance-fee work-from-home scams (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Bank/Airline Customer Care Number via Search Engine
script_summary: Victim searches online for a bank, airline, or company's customer care number and calls a fraudulent number planted high in search results or on a fake listing. The fraudster then guides the victim through 'verification' steps that lead to OTP theft or remote-access installation.
red_flags: customer care number found via general web search rather than the company's official app or statement
call handled by someone who quickly asks for OTP, PIN, or remote access
number not matching the one listed on the company's verified official website
resolution offered unusually quickly for a complex complaint
source: Cybercrime cell and bank advisories warning against searching for customer care numbers online (recurring, 2022-2025)

title: ATM Card Skimming/Cloning
script_summary: Fraudsters install a hidden skimming device and camera on an ATM to capture card data and PIN entry. The captured data is used to clone the card and withdraw funds, often from a different city, shortly after the victim's legitimate transaction.
red_flags: ATM card slot or keypad appears loose, bulky, or different from usual
unusual attachment or reflective object near the keypad that could hide a camera
unexplained withdrawals from distant locations shortly after ATM use
difficulty inserting or removing the card at the machine
source: Bank and RBI advisories on ATM skimming prevention (recurring, longstanding pattern)

title: Fake Traffic Police E-Challan SMS Scam
script_summary: Victim receives an SMS claiming a traffic violation e-challan is pending, with a link to 'pay' the fine that leads to a phishing page mimicking the Parivahan or state traffic police portal, capturing card details and OTP.
red_flags: link domain does not match the official parivahan.gov.in or state police portal
request for full card details and OTP to pay a minor traffic fine
urgency framed around vehicle registration suspension
message sent from an unofficial personal or unlisted number
source: State traffic police and cybercrime advisories on fake e-challan phishing SMS (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Digital Arrest Variant — NCB Narcotics Parcel Escalation
script_summary: Following an initial courier-scam call, the victim is connected to someone claiming to be from the Narcotics Control Bureau, alleging the intercepted parcel contained banned substances, and demanding a 'clearance deposit' held under digital arrest to avoid arrest and media exposure.
red_flags: impersonation of NCB specifically tied to a parcel narrative
threat of public/media exposure to increase pressure
demand for a 'clearance deposit' framed as fully refundable
continuation of the courier-scam escalation pattern into a digital arrest
source: Consistent with courier-scam escalation pattern reported across multiple 2023-2024 Deccan Herald and police cyber cell cases

title: Digital Arrest Variant — Fake CBI Summons via WhatsApp
script_summary: Victim receives a WhatsApp message containing a forged CBI or police summons document, instructing them to join a video call within a set time or face immediate arrest, after which the standard digital arrest extortion script follows.
red_flags: official summons delivered via WhatsApp rather than any formal legal channel
forged letterhead document used to create legitimacy
compressed deadline to join a video call under threat of arrest
continuation into standard digital-arrest fund-transfer demands
source: Synthetic — modeled on the documented digital-arrest pattern with a WhatsApp-delivery variation noted in cybercrime awareness materials

title: Fake Court Summons Delivered by Email or Post
script_summary: Victim receives a forged legal notice or summons by email or courier, referencing a fabricated case number, and is instructed to call a number on the document to 'resolve' the matter before a hearing date, leading into the same extortion pattern as digital arrest.
red_flags: legal document received through an unofficial email address rather than a court's e-filing system
instructs calling a personal mobile number rather than visiting the court in person
fabricated case number that cannot be verified on official court websites
leads into demand for money to 'settle' or 'withdraw' the case
source: Pattern consistent with reported forged-court-order digital arrest cases (2024-2025)

title: Fake Aadhaar-PAN Linking Update Scam
script_summary: Victim receives an SMS or call claiming their PAN card will be deactivated unless Aadhaar linking is 'updated' immediately through a provided link, which captures Aadhaar number, PAN, and OTP on a fraudulent page.
red_flags: urgency around PAN deactivation with a strict deadline
link domain does not match the official incometax.gov.in or UIDAI portal
request for OTP alongside Aadhaar and PAN details
message arrives from an unofficial or masked sender ID
source: Income Tax Department and UIDAI advisories on Aadhaar-PAN linking phishing patterns (recurring each linking deadline cycle)

title: Vishing — Fake Bank KYC Verification Call
script_summary: Fraudster calls posing as a bank employee, states the account will be suspended unless KYC is verified over the phone, and guides the victim to share OTP, card number, or CVV under the pretext of 'confirming identity'.
red_flags: inbound call claiming to be the bank rather than the victim contacting the bank directly
request for OTP, CVV, or full card number over a phone call
urgency around account suspension within hours
bank staff are never required to ask for OTP or CVV by phone
source: RBI and bank advisories reiterating banks never ask for OTP/CVV over calls (recurring, longstanding advisory)

title: Smishing — Fake Bank Link SMS
script_summary: Victim receives an SMS appearing to be from their bank warning of suspicious activity, containing a link to a phishing page designed to look like the bank's login portal, capturing net-banking credentials and OTP.
red_flags: SMS link domain does not match the bank's official website
warning of suspicious activity with a link to 'secure' the account
page requests net-banking username, password, and OTP together
sender ID is a generic or unofficial short code
source: Bank and telecom operator advisories on recurring SMS phishing (smishing) campaigns (2022-2025)

title: Fake 'Refund Processing' Callback Scam
script_summary: Victim who previously made an online complaint or refund request receives a follow-up call from someone posing as support staff, asking to 'confirm' bank details or install an app to 'process' the refund faster, which instead enables unauthorized withdrawal.
red_flags: unsolicited follow-up call referencing a genuine but unrelated past complaint
request to install an app or share OTP to 'speed up' a refund
refund amount and process do not match what the original company confirmed
caller pressures for immediate action during the call itself
source: Pattern consistent with broader refund-impersonation scams reported by consumer and bank fraud advisories

title: Fake Matrimonial Profile — Advance Visa/Travel Fee Scam
script_summary: Fraudster builds trust through a matrimonial site profile claiming to live abroad, then asks the victim to send money for a visa, flight ticket, or travel emergency to finally meet in person, after which contact stops once payment is received.
red_flags: prospective match living abroad who avoids video calls or in-person meeting for a long period
requests money specifically tied to visa fees, flight tickets, or travel emergencies
profile details cannot be independently verified through the matrimonial platform's verification tools
urgency framed around a 'last chance' to finally meet
source: Consumer fraud advisories on matrimonial-site financial scams (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Study-Abroad/Education Consultant Scam
script_summary: Victim pays a large upfront fee to an unregistered education consultant promising guaranteed university admission or visa approval abroad, after which the consultant becomes unreachable or the promised admission/visa never materializes.
red_flags: guarantee of admission or visa approval, which no legitimate consultant can promise
large upfront fee demanded before any application is filed
consultant not listed or verifiable with any recognized regulatory or university body
pressure to pay quickly to 'lock in' a supposedly limited seat
source: Consumer protection advisories on education-consultant fraud reported in Indian regional media (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Property/Real Estate Booking Scam
script_summary: Victim is shown an attractively priced property listing (often for a plot or flat) and asked to pay a 'booking amount' quickly to secure it before a supposed rush of other buyers. The property either does not exist, is already sold, or is not owned by the seller.
red_flags: unusually low price compared to similar listings in the same area
urgency to pay a booking amount before viewing documents or the property in person
seller avoids an in-person meeting or site visit
no verifiable title deed or registration documents provided before payment
source: Consumer protection and real estate regulatory advisories on booking-fee property fraud (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Vehicle Insurance Claim Settlement Scam
script_summary: Caller claims to be from the victim's vehicle insurer, stating a claim can be settled faster or a bonus refunded if the victim shares policy details and pays a small 'processing charge' via UPI, after which the caller disappears.
red_flags: unsolicited call about a claim or bonus the victim did not initiate
request for a processing charge before any claim is formally reviewed
payment requested to a personal UPI ID rather than the insurer's official channel
urgency framed around a limited-time settlement offer
source: IRDAI advisories on insurance claim-settlement impersonation scams (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Government Job Offer — Registration Fee Scam
script_summary: Victim receives a message or call offering a government job (railways, police, postal service) contingent on paying a 'registration', 'security deposit', or 'document verification' fee, after which no job materializes.
red_flags: any fee requested for a government job application or offer letter
offer received via WhatsApp or personal call rather than an official recruitment portal
urgency to pay before a supposed vacancy closes
recruiter cannot be verified through the department's official recruitment board
source: Government recruitment boards and cybercrime advisories on fake job-offer fee scams (recurring pattern)

title: Fake Charity/Disaster Relief Donation Scam
script_summary: Following a real natural disaster or crisis event, fraudsters set up fake fundraising pages or messages soliciting donations, using urgency and emotional appeals, with funds going to personal accounts rather than any verified relief organization.
red_flags: fundraising appeal appears immediately after a disaster with no verifiable registered NGO behind it
donation requested to a personal UPI ID or account rather than an official relief fund
emotional imagery used to pressure quick donation without scrutiny
no transparency on how funds will be used or accounted for
source: Consumer protection advisories on disaster-relief donation fraud, a recurring pattern after major crisis events

title: Missed Call / Call-Back OTP Extraction Scam
script_summary: Victim receives a missed call from an international or unknown number and calls back, or receives a call asking them to read out an OTP just received for an unrelated reason, resulting in unauthorized access to an account or service.
red_flags: unsolicited missed call from an unfamiliar or international number
caller directly asks the victim to read out a code just received via SMS
no legitimate service ever needs a customer to read an OTP over a call
call-back to an unknown number can also trigger premium-rate charges
source: Telecom operator and cybercrime advisories on missed-call and OTP call-back scams (recurring pattern)

title: SIM KYC Block Threat Delivered via Courier Call
script_summary: Combines courier and telecom impersonation: caller claims a SIM card registered in the victim's name is linked to a parcel scam case, and the SIM will be blocked unless the victim 'cooperates' by sharing personal and banking details over a video call.
red_flags: combines a fake parcel narrative with a SIM-block threat
requests banking details framed as necessary to 'verify' SIM ownership
video call used to add pressure and apparent legitimacy
threat of SIM deactivation used as the urgency trigger
source: Synthetic — modeled as a hybrid of the courier-parcel and SIM-related digital arrest patterns documented separately in advisories

title: Digital Arrest — NCB Drug Parcel Combined With Live Video Confinement
script_summary: Extending the courier-to-NCB escalation, the victim is kept on a continuous video call for several hours while 'investigators' review bank statements shared on screen, before demanding a lump-sum transfer framed as bail or clearance.
red_flags: multi-hour continuous video call confinement
victim asked to share screen or documents showing full bank balance
lump-sum demand framed as 'bail' or 'clearance', concepts that do not apply to a phone-based investigation
no opportunity given to consult a lawyer or family member
source: Consistent with the multi-stage courier-to-digital-arrest escalation reported across 2023-2025 Indian cybercrime cases

title: Fake CBI/Police Email With Malicious Attachment
script_summary: Victim receives an email impersonating CBI or police with an attached 'notice' or 'summons' file. Opening the attachment can install malware, or the document itself contains a phishing link requesting personal and banking details.
red_flags: email domain does not match any official government (.gov.in or .nic.in) address
unexpected attachment claiming to be a legal notice or summons
document or embedded link requests personal or banking information
urgent language pressuring the recipient to respond within a short window
source: Cybercrime advisories on phishing emails impersonating law enforcement (recurring pattern)

title: Deepfake/Manipulated Video Call Blackmail
script_summary: Fraudster uses a manipulated or deepfake video during a call, or records a brief innocuous video call and later edits it to appear compromising, then threatens to circulate it to the victim's contacts unless a payment is made.
red_flags: unexpected threat to release a video the victim does not recall being compromising
fraudster is often a stranger who initiated contact shortly before the call
payment demanded quickly under threat of circulation to family or employer
difficulty verifying the authenticity of the threatened footage given editing/deepfake tools
source: Cybercrime advisories highlighting a rise in deepfake-enabled extortion reports (2024-2025)

title: AI Voice Cloning — Fake Distress Call From a Relative
script_summary: Fraudster uses AI-generated voice cloning, built from social media audio/video of a family member, to call the victim sounding like their relative in distress (accident, arrest, kidnapping), urgently requesting money be sent immediately.
red_flags: caller sounds like a known relative but call quality or context feels slightly off
extreme urgency combined with a request to keep the situation secret from other family members
requests immediate money transfer without allowing time to call the relative back directly
background noise or scenario designed to prevent calm verification
source: Cybercrime and consumer advisories flagging emerging AI voice-cloning distress-call scams (2024-2025 reporting)

title: Fake IPO Allotment/Pre-Listing Share Scam
script_summary: Victim is offered guaranteed allotment of a popular IPO or pre-listing shares of an unlisted company at a discount, through an unofficial broker or Telegram group, after paying upfront — the shares or allotment never materialize.
red_flags: guaranteed IPO allotment, which no broker can legitimately promise
unlisted 'pre-IPO' shares offered through unofficial channels rather than a registered broker
upfront payment required before any shares are credited to a demat account
seller cannot be verified against SEBI's registered intermediary list
source: SEBI advisories warning against unregistered pre-IPO and grey-market share sale scams (recurring, 2023-2025)

title: Fake Bank FD Maturity / Locker Renewal Scam
script_summary: Caller claims the victim's fixed deposit has matured or their bank locker agreement needs urgent renewal, and directs them to share OTP or visit a fake 'digital renewal' link to avoid losing access to funds or locker contents.
red_flags: unsolicited call about FD maturity or locker renewal requiring immediate online action
request for OTP or net-banking credentials to 'process' renewal
link provided does not match the bank's official domain
threat of losing locker access or FD proceeds if action is delayed
source: Bank fraud-alert bulletins on FD/locker renewal phishing patterns (recurring pattern)

title: Legitimate One-Time Password (OTP) Message (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: A standard OTP message from a bank or service for a transaction the account holder is actively completing themselves, with a short validity window and an explicit warning never to share the code with anyone — used as a baseline example of legitimate messaging.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate OTP SMS format

title: Legitimate E-commerce Delivery OTP Message (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: A real e-commerce delivery confirmation OTP sent to verify handover of a package the customer actually ordered, with order details matching a purchase the customer recognizes and no request to share the code over a call.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate delivery-OTP SMS format

title: Legitimate Government Scheme Notification (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: An authentic SMS from a verified government sender ID informing a beneficiary of a scheme payment already credited, with no link requesting personal or banking information and no request for any action.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate government scheme SMS format

title: Legitimate Bank Callback Confirmation (Safe Baseline Example)
script_summary: A message confirming a callback the customer themselves scheduled through the bank's official app or verified helpline, referencing a specific ticket number the customer can independently verify — used as a baseline contrast example.
source: Composed for baseline/contrast testing — represents typical legitimate customer-service callback format

title: 'Hi Mom/Dad, New Number' WhatsApp Scam
script_summary: Fraudster messages the victim on WhatsApp from an unknown number claiming to be their child or close relative who has lost their old phone, gradually builds trust in conversation, then requests an urgent money transfer for a bill or emergency.
red_flags: message from an unsaved number claiming to be a relative with a 'new number'
requests to save the new number and communicate only via chat, avoiding a voice call
eventual request for an urgent money transfer framed as a bill or emergency
resistance or excuses when asked to do a quick voice or video call to confirm identity
source: Widely reported 'Hi Mum/Dad' scam pattern flagged by telecom operators and consumer advisories internationally and in India (2023-2025)
